intro: Dryopteris trichotoma, also known as the golden shield fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus, characterized by its unique fronds with golden-brown scales on the underside. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris trichotoma include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are the most common herbal preparations made with this plant, often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues.
